web.title: Swimming Pool Renovation Cost Australia(2025)
web.description: Swimming Pool Renovation Costs by Type in Australia · Resurfacing/Replastering ($4,000 – $15,000 AUD) · Tile Replacement ($3,000 – $12,000 AUD).
web.markdown:
# Swimming Pool Renovation Cost Australia(2025)

![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/b531395948de923ec4723ec5f9d5b1452cbbd9cb31de1abf358a0e347d5efa55?s=55&d=mm&r=g)

[Estate Renovation](https://estaterenovation.com.au/author/rafiestaterenovation/)

•

[Pool Renovation](https://estaterenovation.com.au/category/pool-renovation/)

•

October 14, 2025

![Swimming Pool Renovation Cost Australia(2025)](https://estaterenovation.com.au/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Swimming-Pool-Renovation-Cost-1200x675.jpg)

Planning a pool refresh? The average swimming [pool renovation](https://estaterenovation.com.au/) cost in Australia typically ranges from $5,000 to over $45,000, depending on the scope of your project. For a basic facelift, such as renovate a small pool or replacing a vinyl liner, you can expect to budget between $5,000 and $12,000. Mid-range renovations, which might include retiling, new coping, and minor equipment upgrades, usually fall between $12,000 and $25,000. For a premium overhaul involving structural changes, high-end materials, and advanced features, costs can easily exceed $25,000. The final price varies significantly by state, so exploring costs for NSW, VIC, QLD, WA, and SA is a smart next step. Ready to find out what your project will cost? Get free quotes from local professionals today.

Table of Contents

[Toggle](https://estaterenovation.com.au/pool-renovation-cost-guide-2025/#)

## Why Australian Swimming Pool Renovation Costs Vary by State

The price you pay for a pool makeover isn’t the same across the country. An Australian pool renovation cost is heavily influenced by where you live. Key regional market differences, climate, and contractor availability all play a significant role in the final quote you receive. Understanding these state-by-state variables is crucial for setting a realistic budget.

### Regional Market Differences

Each state and territory has its own economic landscape. Labour rates are a primary driver of cost variation, with major cities like Sydney often having higher trade costs than Adelaide or Hobart. The availability of materials and transport logistics also matters. It costs more to ship premium tiles and heavy equipment to Perth or a remote town in Queensland than it does to a suburb in Melbourne, and this is reflected in the pool upgrade price Australia-wide. Finally, seasonal demand can cause prices to fluctuate, with contractors being busier during warmer months.

### State Comparison Overview

Here’s a brief look at how costs differ across the nation:

- **New South Wales:** Expect high labour costs in a temperate climate.
- **Victoria:** A cooler climate often leads to seasonal work limitations.
- **Queensland:** Year-round demand and high humidity create unique needs.
- **Western Australia:** A hot, dry climate and remote location can add a premium.
- **South Australia:** A growing market with more mid-range costs.
- **Tasmania:** Limited contractor availability often results in premium pricing.
- **Northern Territory:** The extreme climate demands specialist skills, leading to the highest costs.

### Climate Impact on Costs

Australia’s diverse climate directly impacts renovation choices and costs. In tropical northern regions, materials must withstand intense UV exposure and high humidity, while pools in coastal areas need protection against salt air corrosion. In contrast, temperate zones like [Victoria](https://www.visitmelbourne.com/practical-information/about-victoria) may prioritise heating solutions. These climate-specific requirements influence the durability and type of materials chosen, affecting the overall budget.

### Contractor Availability by Region

In major cities, you’ll find a wide selection of contractors, leading to competitive pricing. However, in regional or rural areas, the pool of qualified professionals is much smaller. This limited supply can drive up prices and lead to longer waiting times, as local supply chains may also be less robust. Researching Australian pool costs by region will help you anticipate these differences.

## Swimming Pool Renovation Costs by Type in Australia

Breaking down your project into specific tasks helps clarify where your money is going. The pool renovation cost Aussie homeowners face is very different from the cost of a full equipment upgrade. Here are the typical price ranges for common renovation jobs across Australia.

### Resurfacing/Replastering ($4,000 – $15,000 AUD)

Giving your pool a new interior surface is one of the most effective ways to transform its look and feel. The cost depends heavily on the material chosen, from affordable paint to premium pebble or glass bead finishes. Australian standards dictate the quality and application process, and you’ll find that local materials are often more budget-friendly than imported ones. Durability is key; a slightly more expensive finish may last longer in harsh Aussie conditions, saving you money in the long run. The average cost per square metre varies but is a good metric to use when comparing quotes.

### Tile Replacement ($3,000 – $12,000 AUD)

Replacing cracked, faded, or dated tiles can instantly modernise your pool. This cost typically covers tiling the waterline, but a full interior tiling job will be significantly more. Australian pool tile options range from classic ceramic to luxurious glass mosaics. Labour rates for tilers differ between states, and you must also factor in the cost of high-quality, waterproof grout and sealants. Many popular Australian tile brands offer products designed specifically for local pool conditions.

### Equipment Replacement ($2,000 – $8,000 AUD)

Upgrading your pool’s circulation and filtration system can improve water quality and reduce energy consumption. This includes Australian-spec filter systems (sand, cartridge, or glass media) and modern, energy-efficient pump options. Solar heater installation has become a popular, eco-friendly choice, with costs varying by system size. You can also invest in automation and smart pool systems that allow you to control lighting, heating, and cleaning from your phone. Learn more about equipment costs in \[State\].

### Deck Renovation ($3,000 – $15,000+ AUD)

The area around your pool is just as important as the pool itself. Deck renovations can range from simple concrete resurfacing to laying new pavers or stone. Timber deck alternatives, such as composite decking, are popular for their durability in the Australian climate. Regional preferences often dictate material choices, with non-slip surfaces being a priority everywhere.

### Structural Repairs ($8,000 – $50,000+ AUD)

Structural issues are the most serious and expensive problems to fix. This includes professional crack repair, which requires specialised techniques to ensure a permanent seal. Leak detection and fixing can be complex, often involving pressure testing and excavation. In older pools, foundation issues may need to be addressed. Salt pool conversions are also gaining popularity, requiring an upgrade to salt-compatible equipment.

### Full Renovation ($20,000 – $75,000+ AUD)

A complete overhaul involves gutting the existing pool and starting fresh. This could include changing the shape, depth, and adding major features like a spa or waterfall. Timelines for such projects are extensive, and you will need to factor in costs for building permits and council approval, which vary by location. While a major investment, a full renovation offers a significant return on investment (ROI) by boosting your property value in the competitive Australian market.

## Swimming Pool Renovation Costs by Australian State

Where you live is one of the biggest determinants of your final bill. Here’s a state-by-state breakdown to help you understand the average **swimming pool renovation cost** in your local area.

### New South Wales (Sydney, Newcastle, Central Coast)

- **Average Cost Range:** $8,000 – $50,000+
- **Details:** Labour and material costs in Sydney are among the highest in the country. Popular trends include mineral pool conversions and high-end glass tile finishes. Costs can be slightly lower in regional centres, but coastal areas often demand materials resistant to salt spray.

> Read full guide on: [Pool Renovation cost in Sydney](https://estaterenovation.com.au/pool-renovation-cost-sydney/).

### Victoria (Melbourne, Geelong, Bendigo)

- **Average Cost Range:** $7,000 – $40,000+
- **Details:** The cooler climate means renovation work is often seasonal, peaking in spring and summer. Pool heating is a popular upgrade. Budget-friendly options like vinyl liner replacements are common.

### Queensland (Brisbane, Gold Coast, Cairns)

- **Average Cost Range:** $6,000 – $45,000+
- **Details:** With year-round swimming weather, renovation work is always in demand. Tropical climate factors mean choosing materials that resist mould, fading, and high humidity is essential. Costs vary between \[Brisbane\], the \[Gold Coast\], and \[Cairns\].

### Western Australia (Perth, Fremantle)

- **Average Cost Range:** $9,000 – $55,000+
- **Details:** The state’s remote location can increase material transport costs and lead to extended project timelines. The hot, dry climate demands highly durable surfaces and UV-resistant materials.

### South Australia (Adelaide, regional areas)

- **Average Cost Range:** $6,000 – $35,000+
- **Details:** Adelaide offers more moderate pricing, with a growing market of skilled contractors. Availability can be limited in regional areas, potentially increasing costs.

### Tasmania & Other States (Hobart, Launceston)

- **Average Cost Range:** $8,000 – $40,000+
- **Details:** A smaller pool of available contractors can lead to premium pricing. The cooler climate makes efficient pool heating systems a wise investment.

### Northern Territory (Darwin, Alice Springs)

- **Average Cost Range:** $10,000 – $60,000+
- **Details:** Renovations here require specialists familiar with the extreme climate. Costs are the highest in the nation due to logistical challenges and the need for robust, heat-resistant materials.

| State | Average Cost | Labour Rate | Availability | Climate Factor |
| NSW | $8k – $50k+ | High | High | Temperate/Coastal |
| VIC | $7k – $40k+ | High | Seasonal | Cool/Temperate |
| QLD | $6k – $45k+ | Medium-High | High | Tropical/Humid |
| WA | $9k – $55k+ | High | Medium | Hot/Dry |
| SA | $6k – $35k+ | Medium | Growing | Temperate/Dry |
| TAS | $8k – $40k+ | Medium | Low | Cool/Temperate |
| NT | $10k – $60k+ | Very High | Low | Extreme Heat |

## Pool Cost Breakdown by Australian Region

Beyond the state level, costs also vary between metro, regional, and rural areas.

### Metropolitan Areas

Major cities like Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane have the most competitive markets. While labour costs are higher, you benefit from a wide choice of contractors and faster material availability. This competition can sometimes help keep prices in check for standard jobs. See our \[NSW\], \[VIC\], and \[QLD\] guides for more.

### Regional Centers

In cities like Geelong, the Gold Coast, and Perth, you’ll find a balance. Costs are often mid-range—not as high as in the biggest capital cities but more than in rural towns. You may experience slightly longer timelines for material delivery or contractor availability. Check the relevant \[state guides\] for details.

### Rural and Remote Areas

This is where you’ll see significant price premiums. Material transport costs are a major factor, and the limited number of local contractors means less competition and higher travel fees. Planning well in advance is essential if you live in a remote area.

## Factors Affecting Australian Pool Renovation Costs

Several key elements will influence your total spending.

### Pool Size and Depth (Aussie standards)

It’s a simple equation: a larger and deeper pool requires more materials and labour, increasing the cost. Most projects are priced per square metre for surfacing or per linear metre for tiling and coping. Converting a standard chlorine pool to saltwater will also add to the expense due to equipment changes.

### Existing Pool Condition

A pool with significant cracks, leaks, or “concrete cancer” will cost much more to repair than one that only needs a cosmetic update. A professional damage assessment is often the first step, and some councils may require an inspection, which adds to the initial fees.

### Building Permits & Council Requirements

The need for a permit varies significantly by state and local council. Major structural changes almost always require approval, which involves fees and can add weeks or months to your timeline. Always check with your local council before starting work.

### Seasonal Timing

You may find discounts if you book your renovation during the off-season (winter in most southern states). However, be aware that cold or wet weather can impact the quality of some work, like rendering or painting. Peak seasons in each state often come with higher demand and prices.

### Contractor Type

Always choose a licensed and insured contractor. While a cheaper, unlicensed operator might seem tempting, it can lead to voided insurance and poor workmanship. Getting quotes from both local and interstate contractors can give you a better sense of the market rate.

## Hidden Costs to Expect in Australia

Your initial quote may not cover everything. Be prepared for these potential extra expenses:

- **Council Permits and Inspection Fees:** These vary by state and can add hundreds or even thousands of dollars.
- **Drainage and Water Management:** Ensuring proper drainage is critical in the Australian climate and may require extra groundwork.
- **Electrical Work:** Upgrading lights or pumps requires a licensed electrician to ensure compliance.
- **Chemicals and Balancing:** Your new pool surface will need to be correctly balanced, which involves the cost of chemicals and testing.
- **Landscaping:** The area around the pool may need repairs after heavy equipment has been on-site.
- **Travel Costs:** Contractors may charge travel fees for projects in regional and remote areas.
- **Contingency Fund:** It’s wise to set aside an extra 10-15% of your total budget to cover any unexpected issues.

## FAQ

### How long does pool renovation take in Australia?

A simple renovation might take 1-2 weeks, while a full renovation can take 1-3 months, depending on council approvals and weather.

### Do I need council approval for pool renovation?

It varies by state and the scope of work. Cosmetic updates usually don’t, but structural changes almost always do. Always check with your local council.

### What’s the cheapest pool renovation option in my state?

Generally, replacing a vinyl liner or repainting a concrete pool is the most affordable option.

### Is saltwater conversion worth it in Australia?

Yes, for many. Saltwater pools are often gentler on the skin and require fewer chemicals, making them a popular choice despite the initial conversion cost.

### How often should Australian pools be renovated?

This depends on the climate and pool surface. A concrete pool might need renovation every 10-15 years, while a vinyl liner may last 7-10 years. Harsh sun and heavy use can shorten this lifespan.

### Can I renovate my pool in winter?

In many states, yes. Winter is often the off-season, which can mean lower prices. However, cold or wet weather can delay certain tasks.

### Should I renovate or replace my pool?

If your pool has major structural issues, replacement might be more cost-effective. For cosmetic or minor problems, renovation is usually the better choice from an Australian property perspective.

web.title: How Much Does Pool Resurfacing Cost in Sydney (2025 Guide)
web.description: The cost of pool resurfacing in Sydney typically ranges from $5,000 to $25,000+, depending on pool size, chosen finish, existing condition, and ...
web.markdown:
**Sydney, NSW**

**[Pool Building Licence: 483576C](https://verify.licence.nsw.gov.au/details/Contractor%20Licence/1-4D75Y5Z)**

![pool resurfacing in Sydney](https://superiorpools.com.au/images/resource/swimming-pool.jpg)

# Pool Resurfacing Costs in Sydney: 2025 Pricing Guide

- [Home](https://superiorpools.com.au/)
- [Blog](https://superiorpools.com.au/blog/)
- Pool Resurfacing Cost

![pool resurfacing cost Sydney](https://superiorpools.com.au/images/blog/pool-resurfacing-sydney.jpg)

**9 May 2025**

Pool resurfacing is a critical maintenance procedure that extends your pool's lifespan and enhances its appearance. In 2025, Sydney pool owners face various options and costs when considering resurfacing. This comprehensive guide breaks down the current costs, materials, and considerations to help you make an informed decision about your pool renovation project.

> The cost of pool resurfacing in Sydney typically ranges from $5,000 to $25,000+, depending on pool size, chosen finish, existing condition, and additional features. Understanding the different options and their pricing can help you budget effectively for this important home improvement.

## Resurfacing Cost by Material Type

The type of material used for resurfacing significantly impacts the total cost. Here's a breakdown of current costs for different finishes in Sydney:

| Finish Type | Cost per m² | Average Total Cost |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Pebblecrete/Exposed Aggregate | $90 - $150 | $6,500 - $12,000 |
| Glass Beads/Quartz | $120 - $180 | $8,500 - $14,000 |
| Epoxy Paint | $70 - $120 | $5,000 - $7,000 |
| Pool Tiling (Full) | $200 - $400 | $15,000 - $25,000+ |
| Fibreglass Coating | $110 - $170 | $7,500 - $12,000 |

## Comparison of Resurfacing Options

Each pool finish option offers unique benefits and considerations:

### Pebblecrete/Exposed Aggregate

- Popular in Sydney for its durability and non-slip properties
- Wide range of colour options and aggregate sizes
- Typical lifespan: 15-20 years
- Medium maintenance requirements
- Good value for money with excellent longevity

### Glass Beads & Quartz Finishes

- Premium option with brilliant visual effects
- Reflects light for enhanced water sparkle
- Smoother underfoot than traditional pebblecrete
- Typical lifespan: 15-20 years
- Higher initial cost but excellent durability

### Epoxy Pool Paint

- Most economical short-term option
- Quick application and cure times
- Variety of colour choices
- Typical lifespan: 5-7 years
- Best for pools needing frequent colour changes

### Pool Tiling

- Premium luxury finish with endless design possibilities
- Exceptionally durable and easy to clean
- Porcelain, ceramic, and glass options available
- Typical lifespan: 25+ years
- Highest initial cost but longest lifespan

### Fibreglass Coating

- Modern option with smooth finish
- Creates impermeable barrier
- Algae-resistant properties
- Typical lifespan: 10-15 years
- Mid-range cost with good performance

## Additional Cost Factors

Beyond the base resurfacing costs, several other factors may influence your total project expense:

### Surface Preparation Requirements

- Extensive crack repairs: $800 - $3,500
- Structural repairs: $2,500 - $8,000+
- Surface acid washing and preparation: $600 - $1,200
- Old surface removal: $1,500 - $3,000

### Additional Features and Upgrades

- Waterline tile replacement: $1,800 - $4,000
- Steps and bench resurfacing: $800 - $2,000
- Feature lighting integration: $1,200 - $3,000
- Spa integration: $2,500 - $5,000+

| Pool Size | Basic Resurfacing | Premium Resurfacing |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Small (up to 20m²) | $5,000 - $8,000 | $12,000 - $15,000 |
| Medium (20-40m²) | $8,000 - $12,000 | $15,000 - $20,000 |
| Large (40m²+) | $12,000 - $18,000 | $20,000 - $30,000+ |

## Signs Your Pool Needs Resurfacing

Knowing when to resurface your pool can help prevent more costly structural damage. Look for these warning signs:

- Surface roughness or "etching" that catches swimwear
- Visible staining that doesn't respond to cleaning
- Recurring algae problems despite proper chemical treatment
- Cracks or crazing in the surface
- Flaking, peeling, or delamination of previous surfaces
- Leaking or unexplained water loss
- Surface is over 15 years old

## Cost-Saving Tips for Pool Resurfacing

1. **Schedule during off-peak season:** Many pool companies offer lower rates during autumn and winter months
2. **Bundle with other renovations:** Combine resurfacing with other pool upgrades for package pricing
3. **Proper preparation:** Ensure your pool is properly drained and cleaned before contractors arrive
4. **Preventative maintenance:** Regular maintenance can extend the life of your new surface
5. **Consider long-term value:** Higher upfront costs for premium materials often translate to longer lifespans and better value

> Choosing the right resurfacing option involves balancing initial costs with long-term value. While premium materials like tiles or glass bead finishes have higher upfront costs, their extended lifespan and reduced maintenance often provide better value over time. Consider your pool usage patterns, aesthetic preferences, and maintenance willingness when selecting a surface.

## The Resurfacing Process

Understanding the resurfacing process helps you prepare for the project timeline:

1. **Initial assessment and quotation:** 1-2 days
2. **Draining and preparation:** 1-3 days
3. **Surface removal and repair:** 2-4 days
4. **New surface application:** 1-5 days (depending on material)
5. **Curing time:** 3-7 days
6. **Filling and chemical balancing:** 1-2 days

Total project time typically ranges from 7-21 days depending on weather conditions, pool size, chosen finish, and any additional renovations.

## Get a Personalised Quote

Every pool is unique, and resurfacing costs can vary based on your specific requirements and pool condition. For an accurate quote tailored to your pool, contact our expert team today. We offer comprehensive site assessments and detailed estimates that reflect your pool's specific needs.

[Request Free Quote](https://superiorpools.com.au/blog/pool-resurfacing-cost-sydney#)

web.markdown:
WHAT'S THE COST OF A SWIMMING POOL IN AUSTRALIA IN 2025? — THREE BIRDS RENOVATIONS

[![THREE BIRDS RENOVATIONS](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/1550019282983-2ZW48QOJL4D16CMKWEON/THREE-BIRDS-BLACK_TM.png)](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/)

# WHAT'S THE COST OF A SWIMMING POOL IN AUSTRALIA IN 2025?

You can almost feel it now... the sun on your skin, the cool water lapping around you, the sound of kids laughing (or the sweet serenity of having it all to yourself). A pool is the ultimate Aussie backyard upgrade, giving you a water view, even if you live nowhere near the ocean and turning hot summer days into mini holidays without leaving home.

[![Three Birds Renovations House 17, a stylish backyard oasis featuring a clear blue pool, wooden pergola, outdoor bar, sun loungers, and a large beige umbrella surrounded by lush greenery and modern landscaping.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/5c3a699c-fb54-4a83-9308-b4ebca8e4168/241009THREEBcremorne_X4A6691.jpg)](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/get-the-look-house-17-pool)

Three Birds [House 17 Pool](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/get-the-look-house-17-pool) and Alfresco

But before you start ordering those aesthetic inflatable rings and planning your first pool party, let’s talk about the not-so-glam part - how much it actually costs to put in a pool in Australia.

Well, here’s the splash of reality:

## LET’S START WITH A WATCH-OUT ON SWIMMING POOL PRICES

We don't want to drown your dreams before you even start planning, but it pays to be prepared! When you get that first pool quote, nine times out of ten it’s literally just the shell. That sparkling body of water you’re imagining is only part of the story. All the dreamy extras, like fancy waterline tiles, glass pool fencing, paving, heating, and lush landscaping, can add tens of thousands to your total budget. Plus, initial quotes often exclude hidden install costs, such as an estimate for excavation, which can often blow out and lead to a much higher final price than expected.

In many cases, once you include the full pool project (excavation, surrounds, fencing, and landscaping), the final price can be almost double the base pool cost.

## HERE’S WHAT WE’LL COVER:

01. [The Average In-Ground Pool Cost In Australia](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/blog/whats-the-cost-of-a-swimming-pool-in-australia-in-2025#step1)

02. [Pool Types: What Are Your Options?](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/blog/whats-the-cost-of-a-swimming-pool-in-australia-in-2025#step2)

03. [How Much Does A New Swimming Pool Cost?](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/blog/whats-the-cost-of-a-swimming-pool-in-australia-in-2025#step3)

04. [Where Does Your Pool Budget Go?](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/blog/whats-the-cost-of-a-swimming-pool-in-australia-in-2025#step4)

## THE AVERAGE IN-GROUND POOL COST IN AUSTRALIA

Our most current industry knowledge shows that the average cost to install an in-ground pool in Australia in 2025 now sits between **$60,000 and $80,000**. Rising material and labour costs (hello, concrete, steel, and tradie wages!) have pushed prices up around 25–50%in recent years, but the final figure depends heavily on the type of swimming pool, its size, site conditions, and any extras you choose. While you can still find smaller fibreglass plunge pools from around $35,000–$45,000 in some regions, that’s the exception, not the rule. For most families in metro areas, a realistic budget starts at $60k+.

## POOL TYPES: WHAT ARE YOUR OPTIONS?

When it comes to building a swimming pool, the first big decision is choosing the right pool type and materials for your backyard and lifestyle. The three most popular options in Australia are concrete and fibreglass pools - each with its own unique benefits and considerations.

**Concrete pools** are the go-to choice for those who want complete design freedom. You can customise the shape, depth, and size to fit any space or vision, making them perfect for tricky sites or statement pools. However, this flexibility comes at a price: concrete pools generally have higher initial costs and require more ongoing maintenance to keep the concrete surfaces looking their best.

That said, many people are opting for pre-fabricated concrete plunge pools, which are dropped in, much like fibreglass pools. These can't be customised, like a concrete pool that's poured on-site, but offer the look of concrete for those who want it at a cheaper price point.

**Fibreglass pools** are a favourite for their durability, smooth gelcoat surface, and low maintenance needs. These pools come in a range of pre-moulded shapes and sizes, making installation quicker and often more affordable than concrete. Innovations like Compass fibreglass ceramic pools offer even greater strength and longevity, making them a smart choice for many families looking for a new swimming pool that’s easy to care for.

Ultimately, your choice of pool type, whether it’s a concrete or fibreglass pool, will impact not just the initial price but also the maintenance, longevity, and overall enjoyment of your new swimming pool. Consider your budget, desired pool size, and ongoing maintenance costs before making your decision.

## HOW MUCH DOES A NEW SWIMMING POOL COST?

Are you thinking of a [petite plunge pool](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/blog/why-you-need-a-plunge-pool-in-your-life) or a full-length lap pool? A fibreglass pool or a fully customised concrete pool? There is a wide variety of pools sold in Australia, each with its own associated costs.

Here’s a guide to what you’ll get for your splash of cash (remember, this is for the pool only and doesn’t include surrounds like fences or landscaping):

- **Budget: $30,000–$45,000**

Entry-level fibreglass or above-ground pools with standard finishes and basic filtration. More common in regional areas or very compact sites.

- **Standard: $45,000–$70,000**

Mid-sized fibreglass or concrete pools covering an “average backyard” brief, with paving, fencing, and upgraded filtration.

- **Premium: $70,000+**

Fully customised concrete pools with luxe features like heating, spas, or architectural landscaping. High-end designs like infinity pools easily push into six figures.


When considering concrete pools, note that concrete pools start at similar price points to fibreglass pools but can increase significantly with custom features and design options.

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/5b09e4f2-5134-4268-9ead-1ef630d70e71/Dollar-Sign-illo-1.jpg)

## WHERE DOES YOUR POOL BUDGET GO?

### **The Shell**

Whether you choose fibreglass or concrete, the shell is typically supplied by a manufacturer or builder, and it’s your biggest upfront cost. The manufacturer’s pool shell technology can significantly impact both the cost and quality, with innovations like fibreglass ceramic layers offering enhanced durability and performance. Fibreglass pool manufacturers usually provide a range of shell options, each with different features, warranties, and installation timelines. Concrete pools are generally more expensive but give you endless design freedom when it comes to size, shape, and finish.

**Popular Interior Finishes For Concrete Pools:**

**Pebblecrete** incorporates small pebbles or stones into the plaster, creating a textured, slip-resistant surface. Pebblecrete offers a natural, organic look and is known for its durability.

**Tiles** offers an almost limitless range of design possibilities, with options including ceramic, porcelain, glass, and stone. It’s durable, easy to clean, and can be highly customised to suit your style.

**Glass bead** finishes create a sparkling, iridescent effect in the water. They’re smooth underfoot, resistant to staining, and can be mixed with other aggregates for unique colour and texture combinations.

💡 _Tip: The interior finish you choose not only affects the look and feel of your pool but also impacts maintenance, lifespan, and cost - so factor all three into your decision._

[![Three Birds Renovations House 10, a modern white two-story home with arched windows, white balustrades, lush greenery, and a swimming pool in the foreground.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/1565235067444-T1VOGCCOPUEZE90SHA23/Pool)](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/getthelookpoolpavilion)

[Three Birds House 10 pool](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/getthelookpoolpavilion) lined with pebblecrete

[![Three Birds Renovations House 17, outdoor living area with rustic timber pergola, sliding glass doors, modern pool with tiled edge, and lush garden landscaping.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/1744075784940-BTMANGT7X4E2N91GTVN2/241009THREEBcremorne_X4A6677.jpg)](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/get-the-look-house-17-pool)

[Three Birds House 17 pool](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/get-the-look-house-17-pool) with tiled swim-out and surrounds

[![Three Birds Renovations House 13, curved swimming pool with glass fence, sun loungers under black umbrellas, and scenic views of open fields and forest.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/1618478877872-BGFUIXWOWQ4VZFIJ4Z8N/201208THREEBannan-5070.jpg)](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/getthelookpoolbackyardhouse13)

[Three Birds House 13 pool](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/getthelookpoolbackyardhouse13) lined in glass bead

### **Excavation, Site Preparation & Pool Installation**

Flat blocks are cheaper. Sloping or rocky sites can add thousands for rock removal, retaining walls, or tight access excavation.

If you like the sound of a fibreglass pool or a pre-fabricated concrete plunge pool, don't forget the costs associated with getting the pool off the truck and into position, which usually requires a crane, and sometimes a very large and expensive one!

### **Filtration & Plumbing**

Every pool needs a pump, filter, and plumbing. Modern pools may include advanced operating systems, such as integrated filtration, circulation, and cleaning features. Popular choices include saltwater, chlorine, and [mineral systems](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/blog/mineralpoolconversion). High-quality operating systems can be a key benefit, reducing maintenance and improving water quality. For cleaning, robotic cleaners are the most popular option. If you're feeling fancy, in-floor cleaning systems are a hassle-free way to keep your pool looking pristine every day without thinking about it.

### **Heating & Covers**

Heating can extend your swimming season but comes with extra upfront costs. Solar, gas, or heat pumps - choose based on your climate and usage (speak to your pool company for recommendations based on your location). Heat pumps are a popular and efficient choice for many pool owners.

In some states, including New South Wales, a compliant pool cover is a BASIX requirement for new pools to help reduce evaporation and improve energy efficiency, so be sure to factor this into your budget and design plans from the start. Even if it’s not mandatory where you live, a good-quality pool cover can save you money on heating, cleaning, and water top-ups over time.

### Pool Fencing

It’s a legal must-have in Australia and, depending on the look of it, can be a decent chunk of your budget. Glass fencing is a popular choice for its sleek, unobtrusive look that lets your pool be the star of the show, but it does come with a higher price tag and extra cleaning to keep it crystal clear. Traditional aluminium fencing is the most budget-friendly option - it's functional, durable, and available in a range of powder-coated colours, while custom steel designs are the ultimate splurge, offering a bespoke look that can double as a design feature in your outdoor space.

| Fencing Type | Approx. Cost (per metre) | Pros | Cons |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Aluminium | $200 – $350 | Most budget-friendly, durable, low maintenance, variety of colours | Not as luxe, more visually obtrusive |
| Glass (frameless) | $450 – $650 | Sleek, modern, unobtrusive view of the pool | Higher cost, frequent cleaning to remove water spots |
| Custom Steel | $600 – $900+ | Bespoke designs, high-end aesthetic, can become a feature piece | Most expensive, longer lead times, requires ongoing maintenance |

💡 _Tip: Mixing materials — like aluminium for less-visible sides and glass for the main poolside view — can save thousands without compromising style._

### **Surrounds & Landscaping**

Paving, decking, lighting, and plants all finish the look of your pool area, and can make just as big an impact as the pool itself. Landscaping costs are a bit like asking _“how long is a piece of string?”_ They can vary by tens of thousands depending on factors like the size of your outdoor space, the depth and style of garden beds, the type and maturity of plants, and whether you’re adding extras like a pool house, pergola, or outdoor kitchen.

💡 _Tip: If your budget’s tight, invest in the hardscaping (paving, decking, retaining walls) first, and you can always add more plants and features over time._

[![Three Birds Renovations House 16, elegant outdoor pool area with striped cushions, glass fencing, manicured greenery, and a sunlit conservatory-style extension framed by trees.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/1708563903230-LVQ4YLS54KQ8WIQN3W3Q/231013THREEBglen_X4A2034.jpg)](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/get-the-look-house-16-pool-cabana-back-of-house)

Erin’s established gardens around the [pool at House 16](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/get-the-look-house-16-pool-cabana-back-of-house)

### **Luxury Extras**

Built-in spas, swim-up bars, fancy water features, and in-floor cleaning systems - these luxury extras can take your pool from “nice” to _next-level_. Not only do they enhance your enjoyment (hello, instant holiday vibes), but they can also add serious value to your property. Just keep in mind, each dreamy extra comes with its own price tag and ongoing maintenance, so it’s worth prioritising the features you’ll actually use and love.

## WHO YOU'LL NEED ON YOUR TRADIE TEAM

Installing a pool in Australia is a multi-tradie project. For that reason, many people go straight to a pool company that can coordinate everything you need - from design and pool installation to organising trades to get it hooked up and running, and even testing the water before your first swim. You can choose to coordinate this yourself but, if you're not experienced in construction project management, it can be an overwhelming task.

This dream team might include:

- Licensed pool builder (essential for ensuring your pool is installed safely and meets all regulations)

- Pool builder/installer

- Excavator operator

- Electrician

- Plumber

- Landscaper

- Pool fencing contractor


Choosing a licensed pool builder is crucial, as they bring expertise and help avoid costly mistakes or safety issues. Professionally installed fibreglass pools offer greater safety, reliability, and value compared to DIY options.

Labour often accounts for 40–50% of your total cost but it's worth every dollar for safety, compliance, and longevity.

[![Three Birds Renovations House 10, construction site with workers building a concrete pool foundation, surrounded by timber supports, soil piles, and forest backdrop.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/9edc2726-fbe2-483b-81e4-828908f98a4f/IMG_0938-LR.jpg)](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/getthelookpoolpavilion)

The [House 10 ‘Hinterland Hideaway’ pool](https://www.threebirdsrenovations.com/getthelookpoolpavilion) under construction

## POOL PERMITS & SAFETY COMPLIANCE IN AUSTRALIA

Every swimming pool in Australia must meet strict safety standards. This means you’ll need:

- **Building permits** (usually $500–$2,000+)

- **Fencing certification,** including a compliant CPR sign (mandatory in all states and territories)

- **Inspections** to ensure compliance with Australian Standards AS1926


Rules vary between states, so always check your local Council’s website.

## ONGOING POOL MAINTENANCE & COSTS

Owning a swimming pool is all about fun and relaxation but it’s important to budget for the ongoing costs that come with keeping your pool sparkling and safe. The type of pool you choose will play a big role in how much time and money you’ll spend on maintenance.

**Concrete pools** are known for their strength and customisability, but their porous concrete pool surfaces can attract algae and require more frequent cleaning and chemical balancing. Regular brushing the concrete floor and sides and attention to correct chlorine levels are a must to keep your concrete pool looking its best.

**Fibreglass pools** are a low-maintenance dream thanks to their smooth gelcoat surface, which helps prevent algae and bacteria from taking hold. This means less scrubbing, fewer chemicals, and more time enjoying your pool. Many homeowners find that fibreglass pools minimise ongoing costs compared to other pool types.

Other factors that affect ongoing costs include pool size (bigger pools need more chemicals and energy), the type of filtration and cleaning equipment you choose (robotic cleaners can save time but cost more upfront), and whether you opt for extras like pool heating or hands-free pool care systems.

By understanding the ongoing costs associated with your chosen pool type, you can plan and keep your swimming pool in top condition for years to come.

## DON'T FORGET A BUDGET BUFFER!

Just like with any renovation, set aside an extra 10–20% for surprises - anything from unexpected excavation costs to upgrading to a quieter pump. It sounds like a lot - but chances are you'll need it!

## REAL LIFE RENO: A CONCRETE POOL IN BRISBANE

When Reno Schooler, Amy, moved from a newly renovated house in Sydney to sunny Queensland, the first priority in renovating her new home was to install a pool - crucial for the climate. Having installed a self-cleaning fibreglass pool in Sydney, she was keen to do it again. Her family loved the soft feel underfoot for little feet, the ease of installation, and she loved nothing more than looking out from the kitchen to a sparkling clean pool, thanks to the in-floor cleaning system.

[![Three Birds Renovations Reno School student, Amy Anderson, poolside retreat with turquoise water, striped float rings, lush tropical greenery, and white umbrella creating a resort-style backyard.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/3bc25542-620c-4f12-ae65-cac89f79f9f1/AmyA-Pool-AFTER-IMG_3040-lowres.jpg)](https://www.instagram.com/myislandhome_au/)

[Amy’s New Pool](https://www.instagram.com/myislandhome_au/)

[![Three Birds Renovations Reno School student, Amy Anderson, modern white weatherboard home with upper balcony and poolside courtyard featuring lush greenery and outdoor seating.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/ea82150f-2ef3-4567-8db1-cc445cb87a19/LowRes5A1512.jpg)](https://www.instagram.com/myislandhome_au/)

But a $17,000 quote for a crane to lift a fibreglass pool over her Queenslander house, on a narrow side street that would have to be closed by Council for the duration of the lift (multiple powerlines didn’t help the estimate!), quickly put an end to her fibreglass pool dreams.

When a concrete pool became the only option, she set about designing it to maximise the space available (previously an unused courtyard for the washing line):

- **Size:** 7.6m x 3.3m in-ground concrete

- **Depth:** Shallow end – 1.2m / Deep end – 1.8m

- **Seating bench:** 3.3m x 1m

- **Interior lining:** Premium grade 1–2mm white pebble finish

- **Waterline tiles:** Coconut Ice Glass Mosaics from The Pool Tile Company

- **Lighting:** Multi-coloured underwater pool lights

- **Filtration:** MagnaPool® magnesium mineral pool system

- **Cleaner:** Dolphin M400 robotic cleaner

- **Paving:** Marble pavers from Amber (discontinued)

- **Heating:** Electric heat pump

- **Fencing:** Two sides PVC privacy fence, two sides weatherboard house exterior with white powder-coated aluminium gates


And here’s what the quote looked like:

![Three Birds Renovations Reno School student, Amy Anderson pool works quote, Brisbane 2022, detailing pool construction, upgrades, heating, fencing, landscaping, and total cost of $90,786.99 including GST.](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ab2057d5b409b46dbb5b5ee/dd5878e7-ca8d-404b-9d58-ee95490b57c3/Screenshot+2025-08-13+at+2.06.03%E2%80%AFpm.png)

Actual Quote - Brisbane, 2022

This wasn’t Amy’s first reno rodeo, and while she used a pool company to supply and manage the trades (except for the electrician, which she was required to provide), she was living on site and able to manage the project. Even so, the project blew out to a little over $10k more than the original quote - thank goodness for the contingency budget, which came to the rescue (and was spent in its entirety).

**What blew the budget?** The excavation came in at more than double the provisional allowance. The quote hadn’t included the cost of dumping clean fill, which added extra trips to the tip and additional tip fees. The allowances for the waterline tiling and paving products were also only estimates, and Amy couldn’t source any that aligned with her vision while still falling within the allocated budget.

💡 _Brisbane spotlight:_ the River City is actually one of the cheaper east-coast capitals for pools, but even there, expect to spend $60k–$80k for a basic new pool project in 2025.

## A QUICK REALITY CHECK

So, if you’ve had a pool quote sitting in your top drawer from 2022, brace yourself: the same pool today could cost you an extra $15,000-$25,000. Prices have been climbing steadily with no signs of dropping, so it pays to plan a buffer into your budget and get multiple quotes before you dive in.

Installing a pool in Australia is a major investment, but it can transform your lifestyle and add value to your property.
